evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: build reliable and maintainable data solutions following best practices
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate — reliable, maintainable, best practices, testing, validation — with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: designing scalable analytics solutions that address business and operational needs across markets
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Automotive/digital analytics affecting operational decisions across markets without financial-reporting or regulatory language, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: collaborate with Product, QA, and Engineering teams to validate requirements and ensure stakeholder alignment
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named cross-functional teams (Product, QA, Engineering) and an existing development/deployment process indicate a growing, structured but not fully mature data/analytics practice.
evidence.jd_authorship: owning changes through development, testing, and deployment
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Some named tools and precise process steps (tag management systems, testing, deployment) give partial precision, but bullets like 'understanding of how data is generated and used across systems' are generic, producing a mixed profile.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Candidates need experience in Product Analytics, Digital Analytics, or a technical Data Analyst role, with exposure to behavioral data
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role is framed around product/digital analytics and behavioral data, collaborating primarily with Product and Engineering, placing it in product orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: owning changes through development, testing, and deployment
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs are execution-oriented (design, own, build, collaborate, validate) delivering scoped analytics solutions rather than setting broader strategy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: owning changes through development, testing, and deployment
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing is framed as an owned part of the candidate's development lifecycle responsibility ('owning changes through... testing'), not just a listed skill.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: build reliable and maintainable data solutions following best practices
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: reliability and maintainability are recurring concerns but framed as delivery/engineering quality rather than compliance or stakeholder-trust risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: designing scalable analytics solutions that address business and operational needs across markets
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of designing new analytics solutions (greenfield) and owning changes/maintaining existing systems (fix_scale) with no dominant direction.
evidence.work_arrangement: hybrid/remote work options with remote bonuses
